prompt: Simple sphere: A perfectly round sphere with a radius of 1.0.
Simple box: A basic cube with dimensions 1.0 on all sides.
Simple cylinder: A solid cylinder with radius 0.5 and height 2.0.
Simple torus: A ring shape (donut) with major radius 1.0 and minor radius 0.2.
Simple cone: A standard cone with base radius 1.0 and height 2.0.
Simple capsule: A cylinder with hemispherical caps on both ends.
Rounded box: A cube where all sharp edges and corners are rounded with a small radius.
Hexagonal prism: A solid block with a regular hexagon cross-section.
Triangular prism: A long solid shape with an equilateral triangle base.
Thin sheet: A very thin rectangular plate, much wider and longer than it is thick.
Hollow pipe: A long cylinder with a slightly smaller cylinder subtracted from its center.
Bowl shape: A hemisphere with a slightly smaller hemisphere subtracted from the inside.
Square frame: A square profile hollowed out in the middle to form a picture frame.
Swiss cheese block: A solid cube with multiple spherical holes subtracted from different faces.
Stepped bore: A solid block containing a hole that changes diameter halfway through.
Countersunk hole plate: A flat square plate with a hole that has a conical top and cylindrical bottom.
Counterbored hole plate: A flat plate with a hole that has a wide cylindrical recess at the top and a narrower hole through.
Keyhole slot: A rectangular plate with a hole made of a circle combined with a downward rectangle.
V-groove block: A rectangular block with a triangular trench cut along its top surface.
U-channel: A long rectangular block with a deep rectangular channel running along its length.
Crossed pipes: Two hollow cylinders intersecting at exactly 90 degrees.
Lens shape: The intersection of two slightly overlapping spheres.
Wedge: A triangular block, like a doorstop, formed by slicing a box.
Dice block: A cube with slightly rounded corners and small shallow spherical indentations on the faces.
Crescent moon: A 2D crescent profile extruded into a 3D block, made by subtracting a shifted cylinder.
Step pyramid: Three stacked rectangular blocks decreasing in size from bottom to top.
Hourglass: Two cones meeting at their tips.
Dumbbell profile: A long cylinder with a sphere attached to each end.
Domed cylinder: A short cylinder with a smoothly rounded hemispherical top.
Tapered nozzle: A hollow cone attached to a hollow cylinder of the same outer base diameter.
Star profile: A central cylinder with five pointed triangular wedges arrayed around it.
Pill shape: A short wide cylinder with shallow spherical domes on top and bottom.
L-shaped bracket: Two flat rectangular plates meeting at a 90-degree angle, with rounded outer corner.
T-shaped bracket: Two flat plates meeting to form a T, uniform thickness.
Gusset plate: A triangular plate with three circular holes cut near the corners.
I-beam: A long structural beam with an I-shaped cross-section.
C-clamp body: A thick C-shaped block used as the main frame of a clamp.
Gear blank: A thick disc with a large central hole and a smaller hole offset from the center.
Threaded rod blank: A long thin cylinder with a small chamfer on one end.
Splined shaft blank: A central cylinder with four smaller cylinders evenly spaced around its perimeter.
Cam lobe: A teardrop-shaped extruded profile used for a camshaft.
Flanged bearing: A hollow cylinder with a wide flat ring around one of its ends.
Hex wrench: A long thin hexagonal rod bent at a 90-degree angle.
Chain link side: A flat plate shaped like an oval or capsule with two circular holes.
Rivet head: A very short cylinder acting as a shank with a large smooth dome on top.
Signet ring: A torus that thickens significantly on one side to form a flat square face.
Wheel rim: A torus connected to a central smaller cylinder via four rectangular spokes.
Propeller blade blank: A thin twisted rectangular plate attached to a central spherical hub.
Cone with flat top: A frustum of a cone, where the pointy tip has been cut off flat.
Half pipe: A hollow cylinder sliced perfectly in half down its length.
